questions.q: According to the Puranas, the goddess Sati was the first wife of which great deity?
questions.explanation: Sati is revered in the Puranas as the first consort of Shiva, the great ascetic of Mount Kailash.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the goddess Sati was the daughter of which Prajapati?
questions.explanation: Sati was the daughter of Daksha Prajapati, whose hostility toward Shiva drives her tragic story.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, how did the goddess Sati give up her body during her father's sacrifice?
options: By casting herself into the sacrificial fire
questions.explanation: Unable to bear her father's insult to Shiva, Sati cast herself into the sacrificial fire, as related in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, the goddess Sati married Shiva against the wishes of whom?
questions.explanation: Sati married Shiva against the wishes of her father Daksha, who disapproved of the ascetic god.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the goddess Sati was later reborn as which goddess?
questions.explanation: After giving up her body, Sati was reborn as Parvati, daughter of the mountain king Himavan, to be reunited with Shiva.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the sacred pilgrimage sites that arose where the goddess Sati's limbs fell are known as what?
questions.explanation: Where Sati's scattered limbs fell, the Shakti Pithas arose as sacred sites of pilgrimage, according to the Puranas.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which grief-stricken god carried the body of the goddess Sati across the cosmos?
questions.explanation: Grief-stricken Shiva carried Sati's lifeless body across the cosmos in his sorrow, as told in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the major texts of Hindu mythology, the goddess Sati is honoured as a form of which Great Goddess?
questions.explanation: Sati is regarded in the Puranas as an embodiment of Shakti, the Great Goddess and divine feminine power.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the goddess Sati gave up her body at a grand ritual hosted by her father. What kind of ritual was it?
questions.explanation: Sati gave up her body at Daksha's great sacrifice (yajna), to which her husband Shiva had pointedly not been invited.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the goddess Sati is most associated with which element, reflected in the manner of her end?
questions.explanation: Sati is closely associated with fire, the element into which she cast herself at Daksha's sacrifice.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, why was Shiva deliberately not invited to the great sacrifice of Daksha, father of the goddess Sati?
options: Shiva had insulted the other gods
Daksha bore a grudge and scorned Shiva as an ascetic
questions.explanation: Daksha harboured a grudge and scorned Shiva's ascetic ways, deliberately omitting him from the sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, what did the goddess Sati do when she heard her father was holding a great sacrifice without inviting Shiva?
options: She went to the sacrifice despite Shiva's reluctance
questions.explanation: Sati insisted on attending the sacrifice despite Shiva's reluctance, wishing to confront her father's slight.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the goddess Sati's mother, the wife of Daksha, was named what?
questions.explanation: Sati was born to Daksha and his wife Prasuti, according to the Puranic genealogies.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the divine power that destroyed Daksha's sacrifice in vengeance for the goddess Sati was the fierce being Virabhadra, created by whom?
questions.explanation: Enraged by Sati's death, Shiva created the fierce Virabhadra, who destroyed Daksha's sacrifice.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, what punishment did Daksha receive after his sacrifice was destroyed over the death of the goddess Sati?
options: He was banished to the underworld
He was beheaded and later given a goat's head
questions.explanation: Daksha was beheaded by Virabhadra and, when restored, was given the head of a goat, as related in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which disc-wielding god is said to have cut the body of the goddess Sati into pieces as Shiva wandered in grief, so that the world might be saved from his sorrow?
questions.explanation: Vishnu used his discus to dismember Sati's body, ending Shiva's destructive grief, and the pieces became the Shakti Pithas.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the goddess Sati is counted among the daughters of Daksha. How many daughters is Daksha traditionally said to have had?
questions.explanation: Daksha is traditionally said to have had many daughters, often numbered in the dozens, of whom Sati was the most devoted to Shiva.

questions.q: In the Puranic narrative, the goddess Sati's self-sacrifice is most directly an act of devotion to and defence of whom?
questions.explanation: Sati's act was above all a defence of her husband Shiva, whose honour her father had insulted.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, in what state of mind did Shiva carry the body of the goddess Sati across the worlds?
questions.explanation: Shiva wandered in inconsolable grief and a wild frenzy while bearing Sati's body, threatening the balance of the cosmos.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the goddess Sati's story is part of the larger cycle concerning Shiva's marriages. Which later wife of Shiva continues her devotion in a new birth?
questions.explanation: Sati's devotion continues through Parvati, her rebirth, who again wins Shiva as her husband in the Puranas.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the network of sacred sites linked to the goddess Sati's body are revered especially by worshippers of which tradition?
questions.explanation: The Shakti Pithas, formed from Sati's body, are especially revered in Shaktism, the worship of the Great Goddess.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the goddess Sati is described as performing austerities in an earlier age in order to obtain which boon?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Sati undertook devout austerities so that she might obtain Shiva as her husband.

questions.q: According to the Puranic tradition, the goddess Sati's father Daksha was himself born as a son of which creator deity?
questions.explanation: In the Puranas Daksha is counted among the Prajapatis and mind-born sons of Brahma, the creator.

questions.q: In several Puranic and Tantric traditions, the Shakti Pithas formed from the goddess Sati's body are most commonly numbered as how many principal seats?
questions.explanation: Although counts vary, fifty-one Shakti Pithas are most commonly enumerated, one for each part of Sati's body and her ornaments.

questions.q: According to widely told tradition, the famous temple at Kamakhya in Assam marks the spot where which part of the goddess Sati's body is said to have fallen?
questions.explanation: The Kamakhya Pitha is traditionally venerated as the place where Sati's womb and yoni fell, a foremost Shakti Pitha.

questions.q: In the Mahabhagavata and related Puranas, the goddess Sati is said to have revealed to Shiva her terrifying cosmic forms. These ten goddesses are collectively known as what?
questions.explanation: Sati is said to have manifested the Dasha Mahavidyas, the ten wisdom goddesses, to bar Shiva's path when he forbade her to attend Daksha's sacrifice.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, after Sati's death and the ruin of his sacrifice, Daksha was eventually restored and offered praise to which deity whom he had wronged?
questions.explanation: Once restored, Daksha repented and offered praise to Shiva, the very deity he had insulted, according to the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, the goddess Sati is said to have consumed herself by a fire generated from within through which yogic means?
options: The heat of the desert sun
The fire of her own yogic power (yogagni)
questions.explanation: Several Puranas describe Sati consuming her own body by the inner fire of yoga (yogagni), kindled by her wrath and devotion.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the rebirth of the goddess Sati as Parvati served the larger cosmic purpose of bringing about the birth of which war-god son of Shiva?
questions.explanation: Sati's rebirth as Parvati enabled the union of Shiva and the goddess that produced Kartikeya (Skanda), born to vanquish the demon Taraka.

questions.q: In the textual tradition, the detailed narrative of the goddess Sati, Daksha's sacrifice, and the origin of the Shakti Pithas is most fully preserved in which body of scripture?
questions.explanation: The full story of Sati, Daksha's sacrifice, and the Shakti Pithas is preserved chiefly in the Puranas rather than the earlier Vedic texts.
